summary: The mid-2026 land development landscape across seven counties shows strong builder activity concentrated in Mecklenburg and Gaston, with 15 active builder communities, several featuring substantial single-family developments by Eastwood Homes and D.R. Horton. Cleveland County signals significant high-watch land parcels owned mostly by LLCs, highlighting potential consolidation or large-scale development. Key telecom and utility locate patterns in Mecklenburg, Cabarrus, and Union counties reveal emerging infrastructure projects aligned with industrial and commercial growth corridors, indicating rays of near-future fiber and utility expansion. Zoning and subdivision approvals in Mecklenburg and Gaston reinforce ongoing residential development, suggesting active GIS monitoring and prioritization in these growth nodes. Active land parcels and locate signals in these counties advocate for an integrated strategy focusing on infrastructure readiness, particularly in Cleveland’s high-watch parcels and Mecklenburg’s high-opportunity telecom cluster.
key_findings: Cleveland County has multiple large high-watch parcels (335–739 acres) with watch scores of 9, mostly owned by LLCs, indicating possible consolidation or preparatory stages for development.
Mecklenburg County leads in recent zoning approvals and builder activity, with multiple subdivisions and townhome projects approved, supporting robust residential growth.

Locate patterns in Mecklenburg (Westinghouse Blvd) and Cabarrus (Concord Mills Blvd) counties show very high confidence telecom infrastructure and utility construction signals overlapping high-watch parcels.
Union County shows moderate utility construction signals with no nearby tracked parcels or projects, suggesting early-stage or scattered utility activity.
Builder activity is concentrated in Gaston (Taylor Morrison, Eastwood Homes, D.R. Horton) and Mecklenburg counties, offering varied price points and lot counts predominantly for single-family homes.
top_opportunities: Capitalize on Mecklenburg’s Westinghouse Blvd telecom locate pattern to establish early fiber infrastructure ahead of anticipated industrial growth.
Monitor and pursue development or infrastructure projects in Cleveland’s high-watch parcels owned by multiple LLCs for potential land acquisition or partnership.
Leverage Cabarrus County directional bore signals near Concord Mills Blvd for strategic telecom network expansion to capture emerging commercial demand.
Explore builder collaboration or acquisition opportunities in Gaston County’s final and selling communities, particularly those by Eastwood Homes and D.R. Horton.
Prepare for future utility upgrades in Union County’s South Rocky River Rd corridor to support potential residential and commercial developments.
telecom_insights: Mecklenburg’s high-confidence telecom locate cluster on Westinghouse Blvd is directly associated with four large, high-watch land parcels, indicating active or imminent fiber construction in a key industrial corridor.
Directional bore activity in Cabarrus near Concord Mills Blvd hints at competitive fiber expansion or infrastructure build-out aligned with commercial zone growth.
Union County’s utility construction signal, though moderate and with no tracked parcels, aligns with infrastructure growth corridors demanding ongoing telecom utility readiness.
No active projects currently overlap these telecom locate signals, reflecting potential first-mover advantage for infrastructure providers.
The telecom locate patterns correlate strongly with industrial and commercial development signals, highlighting the need for integrated GIS analysis of land use and network planning.
counties_to_watch: Cleveland — due to high concentration of large, high-watch parcels signaling imminent development or land consolidation activity.
Mecklenburg — driving regional growth with confirmed subdivision approvals, active builder communities, and very high telecom infrastructure signals.
Cabarrus — showing high-confidence telecom expansion signals near major commercial zones with strategic land holdings adjacent.
Gaston — active builder market with new community sales and planning board zoning activity, indicating ongoing residential development.
Union — emerging utility activity indicating early infrastructure investment opportunities linked to growth corridors.
recommended_actions: Initiate detailed GIS parcel and ownership due diligence in Cleveland County’s high-watch LLC-owned lands to assess acquisition or partnership feasibility.
Engage with telecom carriers and utility providers in Mecklenburg and Cabarrus counties to coordinate infrastructure planning leveraging locate pattern insights.
Monitor Mecklenburg County planning boards and permit filings for new residential and mixed-use projects to anticipate builder demand and land acquisition.
Build strategic relationships with prominent builders (Eastwood Homes, D.R. Horton, Taylor Morrison) in Gaston and Mecklenburg to align land offerings with upcoming community developments.
Track Union County utility construction signals for early identification of infrastructure upgrade projects supporting evolving land development patterns.
